name in kana (P1814) == * ウル-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県名張市平尾339', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇流富志禰神社]] {{wikidata link|Q135039260}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined}} in Hirao, {{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was {{jalink|kensha}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 8 ({{jalink|1580|Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{jalink|Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{jalink|Jingokeiun|Q1373049}} 1 ({{jalink|767|Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{jalink|Kashima Jingū|Q706499}} to {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{jalink|hōsai|Q381353}}" and records that {{jalink|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}} site. In '{{jalink|Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 15 ({{jalink|873|Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 1 ({{jalink|1387|Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}, {{jalink|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane||Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}, {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Homusubi-no-Kami}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{jalink|Konpira Daigongen}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{jalink|Ise Jingū|Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{jalink|Emperor Meiji|Q174231}}, {{jalink|Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{jalink|Taga Taisha|Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059}} *{{jalink|Nai Shrine|Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined}} in Hirao, {{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was {{jalink|kensha}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 8 ({{jalink|1580|Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{jalink|Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{jalink|Jingokeiun|Q1373049}} 1 ({{jalink|767|Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{jalink|Kashima Jingū|Q706499}} to {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{jalink|hōsai|Q381353}}" and records that {{jalink|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}} site. In '{{jalink|Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 15 ({{jalink|873|Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 1 ({{jalink|1387|Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}, {{jalink|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane||Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}, {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Homusubi-no-Kami}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{jalink|Konpira Daigongen}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{jalink|Ise Jingū|Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{jalink|Emperor Meiji|Q174231}}, {{jalink|Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{jalink|Taga Taisha|Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059}} *{{jalink|Nai Shrine|Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|kensha}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined}} in Hirao, {{jalink|Nabari|Q166183}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was {{jalink|kensha}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 8 ({{jalink|1580|Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{jalink|Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{jalink|Jingokeiun|Q1373049}} 1 ({{jalink|767|Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{jalink|Kashima Jingū|Q706499}} to {{jalink|Kasuga Taisha|Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{jalink|hōsai|Q381353}}" and records that {{jalink|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}} site. In '{{jalink|Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 15 ({{jalink|873|Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{jalink|Eitoku|Q1145805}} 1 ({{jalink|1387|Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}, {{jalink|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane||Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}, {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Homusubi-no-Kami}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{jalink|Konpira Daigongen}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{jalink|Ise Jingū|Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{jalink|Emperor Meiji|Q174231}}, {{jalink|Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{jalink|Taga Taisha|Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059}} *{{jalink|Nai Shrine|Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill||lt=enshrined|UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank was {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill||lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill||lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane||WD=|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill||lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill||lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill||lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill||lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill||lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill||lt=Konpira Daigongen|UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill||lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane||WD=|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * ウル-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県名張市平尾339', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇流富志禰神社]] {{wikidata link|Q135039260}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane||WD=|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=2025-09}} {{神社 | 名称 = Urufushine Shrine | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=名張市}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}Hirao 3319 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = Unane-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Spring festival / Autumn festival | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Urufu
shine Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Urufushine Shrine''' (うるふしねじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in Hirao, {{ill|lt=Nabari|Q166183|WD=Q166183}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Today, the character for "fu" in Urufushine is commonly written as "冨".{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/|access-date=2025-9-2}} About the origin of the main deity Unane-no-Mikoto, it is explained as deriving from "(Nabari) River meanders => unane."{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=http://urufushine.jp/intro.html#a03|access-date=2025-9-1}} "Benten-iwa" on the Nabari River bank west of the shrine is the sacred body{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine Rock Festival|website=Iwa net|url=https://iwashinbun.com/?special=%e5%ae%87%e6%b5%81%e5%86%a8%e5%bf%97%e7%a6%b0%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-%e5%b2%a9%e7%a5%ad%e3%82%8a|access-date=2025-11-7}}. === History === In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}}), all shrine buildings, treasures, and old documents were lost in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō Iga Rebellion|Q15436846|WD=Q15436846}}. Founding is unknown. The 'Kasuga Shaki' says that in {{ill|lt=Jingokeiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}} 1 ({{ill|lt=767|Q30561|WD=Q30561}}), when Takemikazuchi-no-Kami moved from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499}} to {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}}, he remained here, and this is the origin. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, "Yamatohime Seiki / Jingū-go Chinzaki" says "second year {{ill|lt=hōsai|Q381353|WD=Q381353}}" and records that {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime}} made a circuit to this shrine, indicating it was a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} site. In '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}), Unane deity is Senior Fifth Rank Upper. Later the rank rose gradually, reaching Junior First Rank in {{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|WD=Q1145805}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1387|Q6359|WD=Q6359}}). === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: Unane-no-Mikoto *Deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, Hime Ōkami{{Cite web|title=Urufushine Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/uruhusine-jinja/|access-date=2025-9-27}} === Precinct shrines === *Okitama Matsuo Shrine - deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{Cite web|title=History of Nabari City|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3014146/1/204|access-date=2025-9-1}} *Sannō Gongen-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} *Hachiman-sha - deities: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} *Shiratama Myōjin-sha - deity: Shiratama Myōjin (dragon deity) *Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Atago Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Gokoku Shrine - heroic spirits of those who died for the country *Yasaka Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ichikishima-hime Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Konpira-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira Daigongen|1=UNKNOWN}} *Ōmiwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Nabari City, Lower Volume |publisher=Nabari Local History Research Association |page=370}} === Distant worship sites === {{要出典範囲|Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|WD=Q174231}}, {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship|Q10855625|WD=Q10855625|lt=Imperial Palace distant worship site}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Taga Taisha|Q402091|WD=Q402091}}, distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}|date=2025年10月}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=Tsumita Shrine|Q137691059|WD=Q137691059}} *{{ill|lt=Nai Shrine|Q135039259|WD=Q135039259}} === External links === [http://urufushine.jp/ Urufushine Shrine] official site {{DEFAULTSORT:うるふしねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unanenofushimino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:名張市の歴史]] [[Category:名張市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:伊賀国|社1]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
